element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dragons evolve through item feeding; loyalty system requires player to learn preferred/disliked foods
Players learn issuing general guidelines ('Go!' / 'Wait!') rather than direct commands; unpredictability becomes tactical element
Each squad leader has distinct dragon type; strategic rotation required for varied challenges
Complex balancing: can feed disliked foods for power gains but loyalty penalties; species transformation tied to item types
Overworld navigation; spatial understanding of lagoon-based world map
Story-driven tension escalation; character allegiances shift

typical_relief_triggers: Squad rotation between battles (strategic breathing room)
typical_tension_triggers: Grid-based combat engagement (turn-based but spatially pressurized)
Dragon unpredictability (AI-driven actions outside player direct control)
Item feeding trade-offs (loyalty vs. power optimization)
Battle system described as 'brutal' with trap placements
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical flow with narrative-driven tension escalation; dragon AI unpredictability creates micro-tension within macro strategic planning; campaign progression punctuated by relationship/loyalty complications

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational grid-based combat introduction; single dragon per squad leader; learning curve for turn-based mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Dragon evolution mechanics introduce optimization complexity; multi-squad rotation demands tactical flexibility; balance of loyalty management vs. power escalation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Campaign overrun state (Kahna invasion); increasingly complex betrayals and narrative tension; 'brutal' trap placements in battle design escalate
notable_spikes: Initial transition to dragon AI control (unpredictability spike)
Kahna invasion narrative point (strategic difficulty increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: 2D grid combat encounters as primary spatial structure; 4-character squad formation in turn-based grid system with independent dragon ally
Battle designer Takatsuga Nakazawa known for 'brutal' trap placements; encounters designed to challenge player control and encourage adaptation to dragon unpredictability
Campaign across multiple sky-floating continents ('lagoons'); overworld navigation between tactical battle zones; setting inspired by flying landscape perception

sources_used: Wikipedia: Bahamut Lagoon (gameplay, development, battle system design, dragon mechanics)
